About: It is about four distinct royal houses: the Angevins, the main line of the Plantagenets, and the Houses of Lancaster and York.
Affects: It affects the English throne from 1154 until 1485.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the French county of Anjou.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the history of England.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the Angevins, the main line of the Plantagenets, and the Houses of Lancaster and York.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Geoffrey II, Count of Gâtinais and his wife Ermengarde of Anjou.
FoundedIn: It was founded in the French county of Anjou.
HasA: It has a history of transforming England.
It has defined duties to the realm, underpinned by a sophisticated justice system.
It has a family name adopted in the 15th century.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the adoption of Middle English as the language of governance.
It is characterized by popular revolts triggered by the denial of numerous freedoms.
It is characterized by the rivalry between the Houses of York and Lancaster.
It is characterized by the end of the English Middle Ages.
Influenced: It influenced the development of a sophisticated justice system.
It influenced the adoption of Middle English as the language of governance.
It influenced the Wars of the Roses.
It influenced the rise of the Tudor dynasty.
It influenced the centralization of English royal power.
It influenced the advent of early modern Britain.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by power struggles between rulers in northern and western France.
It was influenced by the marriage of Geoffrey of Anjou and Empress Matilda.
It was influenced by the annulment of Louis VII of France's marriage to Eleanor of Aquitaine.
It was influenced by the denial of numerous freedoms.
It was influenced by the rivalry between the House of Plantagenet's two cadet branches of York and Lancaster.
It was influenced by the death of King Richard III.
IsA: It is a distinct English royal house.
KnownAs: It is known as the Angevins.
It is known as the Houses of Lancaster and York.
It is known as the "Angevin Empire" by historians.
It is known as "espace Plantagenet" by historians.
KnownFor: It is known for holding the English throne from 1154 to 1485.
It is known for transforming England.
It is known for negotiating compromises such as Magna Carta.
It is known for adopting the language of the ordinary people, Middle English.
It is known for being defeated in the Hundred Years' War.
It is known for the Wars of the Roses.
It is known for its descendants becoming monarchs of England and the United Kingdom.
NamedAfter: It is named after Geoffrey, Count of Anjou, who had the nickname Plantegenest.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the 10th and 11th centuries.
It occurred during the Wars of the Roses.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the French county of Anjou.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the French county of Anjou.
PartOf: It is part of the history of England.
It is part of the history of France.
UsedAs: It is used by modern historians to identify four distinct royal houses.
It is used as a name for the main line of the Plantagenets.
It is used as a name for the Houses of Lancaster and York.
It is used as a retrospective name for all of Geoffrey's male-line descendants.
It is used as a term for any sovereign or government derived from Anjou.
It is used as a noun referring to any native of Anjou or an Angevin ruler.
It is used as a term for other counts and dukes of Anjou.
It is used as a term for cousins who held the crown of Jerusalem.
It is used as a term for unrelated members of the French royal family.
It is used as a term for the "Angevin Empire".
UsedFor: It is used for emphasizing Richard of York's status as Geoffrey's patrilineal descendant.
It is used for giving further legitimacy to Richard's great-grandson, Henry VIII.
It is used for identifying the Angevins as a distinct English royal house.
It is used for referring to the period of history in which the Angevin kings reigned.
It is used for referring to any sovereign or government derived from Anjou.
It is used for referring to any native of Anjou or an Angevin ruler.
It is used for referring to other counts and dukes of Anjou.
It is used for referring to cousins who held the crown of Jerusalem.
It is used for referring to unrelated members of the French royal family.
It is used for referring to the "Angevin Empire".
UsedIn: It is used in the context of the French county of Anjou.
It is used in the context of England.
It is used in the context of Normandy.
It is used in the context of Jerusalem.
It is used in the context of the Wars of the Roses.
It is used in the context of the Tudor dynasty.
It is used in the context of the Angevin kings.
It is used in the context of the "Angevin Empire".
UsedTo: It is used to identify four distinct royal houses.

Affects: それは1154年から1485年までイングランドの王位に影響を与えました。
AssociatedWith: それはフランスのアンジュー伯領と関連があります。

FoundedBy: それはガティネ伯ジェフリー2世とアンジュー伯エルメンガルドによって創設されました。

それは洗練された司法制度に裏打ちされた、王国に対する明確な義務を持っています。

HasFeatureOf: それは、一般の人々の言語である中英語を統治の言語として採用したことを特徴としています。
それは、数多くの自由の否定によって引き起こされた、一般的な反乱を特徴としています。
それは、ヨーク家とランカスター家の間のライバル関係を特徴としています。
それは、イングランド中世の終焉を特徴としています。
HeldIn: それはイングランドで保持されていました。
Influenced: それは洗練された司法制度の発展に影響を与えました。

InfluencedBy: それはフランス北部と西部の支配者間の権力闘争の影響を受けました。

それは数多くの自由の否定の影響を受けました。
それはプランタジネット家の2つの分家であるヨーク家とランカスター家の間のライバル関係の影響を受けました。
それはリチャード3世の死の影響を受けました。
